name: TMA shared period selection and multi-range analytics
description: Add one shared week/month/year period picker flow for the Telegram Mini App so Home, household list, and household detail stay synchronized on the same selected range while worker analytics adds `date_from` / `date_to` support without breaking existing month callers.

planned: Broaden worker analytics overview/comparison handling with additive `date_from` / `date_to` ranges without breaking auth, existing month callers, or response-envelope rules.
Add one shared TMA period store, formatter set, chip component, and picker route with Telegram BottomButton confirmation while storing `{ granularity, dateFrom, dateTo }`.
Rewire Home, household list, household detail, and shared summary/household smart components to read the selected range from Zustand and refresh together.
Keep budget UI truthful by rendering budget progress only for month selections.
verification: Focused worker tests passed for `test/unit/dto-analytics.spec.ts`, `test/integration/analytics-overview-read.spec.ts`, and `test/integration/analytics-comparison.spec.ts`.
Focused TMA tests passed for `src/test/period.test.ts`, `src/test/period-chip-entrypoints.test.tsx`, and `src/test/period-store-and-picker.test.tsx`.

Final `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope: 'all', repo: 'household-finance-system')` reported `critical` risk with 132 changed symbols, 24 affected symbols, and 23 changed files; the blast radius is expected because the slice touches shared TMA period helpers, shared finance summary cards, household list/detail read paths, and worker analytics query contracts.

Follow-up correction pass passed focused TMA tests for `src/test/period.test.ts` and `src/test/period-store-and-picker.test.tsx`, then reran `./init.sh build` with `OK` and final `./init.sh` with `Done!` after switching week logic to real Monday-Sunday ISO weeks, separating year/value scroll containers, formatting picker dates as `dd/MM/yy`, and keeping `PeriodPickerPage` BottomButton mount/unmount in a dependency-free effect.

notes: The current repo truth only supports monthly budgets; week/year selections must not invent budget semantics.
